commit.message:
Revise BQL spec: finalize grammar from design discussion

Key changes from iterative design review:
- Explicit and/or with parens for precedence (not multiple where pipes)
- Tags use Bloom syntax: tags has #work (not quoted strings)
- Remove before/after sugar — just use < and >
- Remove contains, matches, select (minimal language)
- $page instead of $current
- Strings must always be quoted
- No double negation (parse error)
- count with group returns per-group counts
- Range predicates: this week, last month, etc. (week start configurable)
- Live feedback via 150ms debounce in SPC v v prompt
- Updated all examples to match new grammar

commit.message:
feat: Add S2Shape implementations that wrap geometry views (#79)

This PR implements an S2Shape implementation that sits on a
`GeoArrowGeometryView`, which is a generic view that can represent any
GeoArrow geometry (including WKB) and in general any serialized
representation of geometry that vaguely follows WKB. The theory is that
in a vectorized execution context where we start with WKB and the only
thing we're going to do is create a S2ShapeIndex and then throw it away,
we can at least get away with not copying the WKB and it might be
faster: in contrast, creating an S2Polygon or S2Polyline or S2Loop is
expensive because there is some validation involved and because there is
no mechanism to recover the scratch space of the internal S2Point
vectors.

These are modeled after the S2PointVectorShape, S2LaxPolygonShape, and
S2LaxPolylineShape, which do vaguely the same thing except they use
arrays for internal S2Point storage.

It may be that it's faster to roll through the GeoArrowGeometryView once
and create the S2Point vectors right away (perhaps reusing the scratch
space inside a loop).

Non-oriented loops can be handled by calling `NormalizeOrientation()`
after `Init()` on a polygon shape. This normalizes any WKB "shell" to be
wound counterclockwise on the sphere and any "hole" to be wound
clockwise on the sphere. This might be expensive.

I haven't quite handled "full" shapes or loops yet.

Closes #77

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add domain model entities for TV Looker

Introduce JPA entities and an enum for the TV Looker domain: Actor, Director, Genre, Item, ListFavorite, Reviews, User and TmdbType. Item includes TMDB metadata and many-to-many relations with Genre, Director and Actor; Reviews links Item and User; ListFavorite stores user-owned favorite item lists and maps to Item. Also normalize application.properties header comment spacing.

* Switch to Postgres and update JPA models

Replace MySQL service with Postgres in docker-compose (rename container, expose 5432, remove volume/healthcheck). Add spring-boot-starter-test to pom and enable spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update in application.properties.

Refactor domain entities: replace wildcard jakarta.persistence imports with explicit annotations, add Entity/Table/Id/GeneratedValue and other JPA mappings, adjust @Column definitions, and introduce relationship mappings (ManyToMany, ManyToOne) and cascade/fetch settings. Add Lombok builders/getters/setters/constructors on several models and change ListFavorite.user from UUID to User. Minor Javadoc/formatting tweaks.

* Modified class "Reviews" to "Review"

* test: test de la bd en funcionamiento con JPA

* Add setup/cleanup scripts and QUICKSTART docs

Add interactive setup and cleanup scripts for Windows and Unix (scripts/setup.{bat,sh}, scripts/cleanup.{bat,sh}), plus a scripts/README.md documenting their usage. Add a QUICKSTART.md for a 5-minute onboarding flow and update README.md with detailed installation, Docker/Postgres, and testing instructions. These changes improve developer onboarding by automating environment checks (Java, Maven, Docker), provisioning PostgreSQL via docker-compose, and providing safe cleanup options.

* Refactor JDBC tests; add Postgres volume

Add a named Docker volume (postgres-data) to docker-compose to persist Postgres data; change H2 dependency scope to test in pom.xml so H2 is used only for tests; remove a debug echo from scripts/cleanup.bat. Heavily refactor ReviewPersistenceTest: enable the 'test' profile, add display names, translate and standardize comments and assertion messages to English, consolidate imports, and make various test cleanup/assertion/SQL tweaks to improve clarity and isolation (sequence resets, clearer failure messages, and minor query/assertion adjustments).

* CI: add test reporter & test profile

Run Maven tests using the 'test' Spring profile (H2) and publish JUnit XML results. Updated the verify step to use -Dspring.profiles.active=test and added dorny/test-reporter to upload target/surefire-reports/*.xml (java-junit) with fail-on-error. Also updated workflow permissions to give pull-requests and checks write access so the reporter and status updates can be posted.

* Add Maven profiles for default and test

Introduce Maven profiles to manage Spring profiles: a 'default' profile (activeByDefault) for local development and a 'test' profile that configures tests to run with the 'test' Spring profile (intended for H2/in-memory testing). The test profile also configures the surefire plugin to pass spring.profiles.active=test to test JVMs. Minor formatting whitespace adjusted near the Asciidoctor plugin.

* feat: implementación del motor de decomendaciones  (#41)

* baes interfaces and clases

* Refactor domain models and add data provider/context

Introduce a generic DataProvider and NoDataProviderException, plus a RecommendationContext to register and retrieve data providers at runtime. Convert Interaction to a JPA entity (annotations, relations, CreationTimestamp) and add @CreationTimestamp to Item and User. Update RecommendationEngine signature to use RecommendationContext and new strategy package names, and rename setEvalationStrategy to setEvaluationStrategy. Move strategy interfaces to new packages (removed old strategy packages) and add simple EvaluationStrategy and RecommendationStrategy interfaces. These changes prepare the codebase for provider-based data access and clearer JPA mappings and strategy wiring.

* Refactor model packages and update recommendation API

Move domain models into org.tvl.tvlooker.domain.model.entity and update imports accordingly; relocate RecommendationContext to domain.motor.utils. Introduce ScoredItem data structure and AggregationStrategy interface. Replace EvaluationStrategy with aggregation-focused API: RecommendationEngine now returns List<ScoredItem> and exposes setStrategies/setAggregationStrategy; add HybridRecommendationEngine stub implementing the new API. Update DataProvider to use the new RecommendationContext type. Also update .gitignore to exclude .agents/ and skills-lock.json.

* Ignore .agent and .claude in .gitignore

Add .agent and .claude entries to .gitignore to prevent local agent/Claude-related files from being accidentally committed.

* docs: add recommendation engine pipeline architecture design

* ScoredItem: use Item, add sourceStrategy

Replace the User reference in ScoredItem with Item, simplify and clarify field Javadoc (score, explanation), and add an optional sourceStrategy field for tracking which strategy produced the recommendation. Update imports accordingly and make this a breaking-API change for callers that previously accessed the user field. Additionally, docs updated to change the recommendation engine behavior: if no candidates remain, the engine should throw NoCandidatesAvailableException instead of returning an empty list.

* Add hybrid recommendation engine and config

Wire and implement the hybrid recommendation engine and related plumbing. Adds RecommendationConfig to register HybridRecommendationEngine as a bean, implements recommend flow in HybridRecommendationEngine (validation, candidate filtering, strategy execution with error handling, aggregation, post-processing) and introduces NoRecommendationsAvailableException. Updates AggregationStrategy interface to include aggregate(...) and getAggregationName(). Adds Spring Boot web and Log4j2 dependencies to pom.xml and enables logging/debug settings in application.properties. Also removes CyclomaticComplexity PMD rule and makes a minor javadoc tweak to ScoredItem.

* Add validation and post-processing to hybrid engine

Add input validation, strategy execution docs and deduplication/score checks to HybridRecommendationEngine; introduce AllArgsConstructor and getters/setters. Implement checkDataNotNull() on RecommendationContext and fix class brace in RecommendationConfig. These changes improve robustness by validating inputs, filtering out invalid scored items, and retaining only the highest-scoring duplicate items during post-processing.

* Add opencode GitHub Actions workflow

Introduce a new workflow 'opencode' that runs on issue and pull request review comments. The job only executes when a comment contains or starts with '/oc' or '/opencode', checks out the repo (actions/checkout@v6) and invokes anomalyco/opencode/github@latest using the OPENCODE_API_KEY secret and model opencode/claude-sonnet-4-5. Permissions for id-token, contents, pull-requests, and issues are set to allow the action to operate.

* Add recommendation strategies design doc

Add a comprehensive design document (docs/plans/2026-03-06-recommendation-strategies-and-aggregations-design.md) describing five recommendation strategies (popularity, content-based, item/user collaborative, matrix factorization), three aggregation methods (constant/variable convex combination, Borda ranking), DataProvider architecture, package layout, and implementation/testing roadmap. Update RecommendationContext.java to support the DataProvider pattern: provider registry, cached computed data (with expiration), getData(T), registerProvider, clearCache, and raw data accessors so strategies remain repository-agnostic and can retrieve precomputed structures from providers.

* Add providers and strategies; refactor engine

Introduce a DataProvider framework and refactor the recommendation engine and strategies.

- Add DataProvider (motor.utils) and ItemPopularityProvider; RecommendationContext now registers providers by ID, caches provider results, and exposes getData(providerId, type) and clearCache.
- Update HybridRecommendationEngine to accept and register List<DataProvider<?>>, convert fields to final, and use updated aggregation/strategy naming and flows.
- Add ConstantConvexAggregation (weight-based aggregation) and PopularityStrategy implementations, plus beans in RecommendationConfig (default/primary aggregation selection and popularity strategy bean).
- Rename strategy packages to org.tvl.tvlooker.domain.strategy.recommendation and .aggregation and remove the old domain.data_provider module.
- Update RecommendationEngine interface (remove setters) and application.properties with aggregation type and strategy weight defaults.

These changes modularize data provisioning, add caching for provider results, and enable configurable weighted aggregation of strategy outputs.

* Make aggregation configurable and add engine validations

Refactor recommendation wiring and strengthen engine robustness: add ConditionalOnProperty to enable/disable the PopularityStrategy bean and move aggregation into a configurable aggregationStrategy bean that builds a weights Map from properties. ConstantConvexAggregation no longer relies on field injection or @Component; it accepts a Map<String,Double> in its constructor and validates weights sum to 1.0. Add two new runtime exceptions (InsufficientDataException, InvalidEngineConfigurationException) and update HybridRecommendationEngine to throw them, validate scored-item ranges, deduplicate by keeping the highest score per item, and return results sorted by score. Remove component annotation from PopularityStrategy so beans are driven from configuration, and add recommendation-related properties and documentation to application.properties.

* Add comprehensive unit tests for recommender

Add a suite of TDD unit tests for the recommendation system components:

- src/test/java/org/tvl/tvlooker/domain/motor/HybridRecommendationEngineTest.java: end-to-end pipeline, input validation, strategy failure handling, provider registration, sorting/deduplication, cold-start and performance checks.
- src/test/java/org/tvl/tvlooker/domain/motor/utils/provider/ItemPopularityProviderTest.java: provider behavior, normalization of popularity scores, cache metadata, edge cases and performance on large inputs.
- src/test/java/org/tvl/tvlooker/domain/strategy/aggregation/ConstantConvexAggregationTest.java: weight-based aggregation, normalization when strategies are missing, explanation merging, deduplication, edge cases and performance.
- src/test/java/org/tvl/tvlooker/domain/strategy/recommendation/PopularityStrategyTest.java: popularity-based recommendation behavior, integration with ItemPopularityProvider, ordering, cold-start and null/empty input handling.
- src/test/java/org/tvl/tvlooker/testutil/TestDataFactory.java: test fixtures and helpers used across tests.

These tests exercise functional behavior, edge cases, and basic performance expectations to improve robustness and drive implementation via TDD.

* Update 2026-03-05-recommendation-engine-design.md

commit.message:
Fix Ctrl+C cancellation with spinner active

Replace Start-ThreadJob spinner with System.Timers.Timer approach.
Timer events fire on ThreadPool background threads which are killed
automatically when the process exits via Ctrl+C, eliminating the
orphaned thread that was blocking clean cancellation.

commit.message:
fix: scope ParameterControl query key to referenced params only (#2101)

Changing one dashboard variable triggers unnecessary ES|QL option
refetches for every other variable because the `parameter-options` query
key includes the entire mutable `parameters` array. This fans out
redundant network requests proportional to variable count.

### Changes

- **`ParameterControl.tsx`**: Replace full `parameters` array in query
key with a `useMemo`-derived subset — only parameters whose `\{\{name}}`
tokens appear in the ES|QL query text.

```tsx
// Before
queryKey: ["parameter-options", ..., parameters]

// After
const referencedParams = useMemo(() => {
  const tokens = new Set();
  for (const m of esqlQuery.matchAll(/\{\{(\w+)\}\}/g)) {
    if (m[1]) tokens.add(m[1]);
  }
  return parameters
    .filter((p) => tokens.has(p.name))
    .map((p) => ({ name: p.name, type: p.type, value: p.value }));
}, [esqlQuery, parameters]);

queryKey: ["parameter-options", ..., referencedParams]
```

- **`ParameterBar.tsx`**: Remove `param.value` from the React element
key. The component already handles value changes internally via
`prevParamValue` — including value in the key caused unnecessary
unmount/remount cycles that bypassed the query cache.

### Tests

- Negative: changing `service` does **not** refetch unrelated `env`
options (call count stays 2)
- Positive: changing `service` **does** refetch `env` options when
`env`'s query contains `\{\{service}}` (call count goes to 3)

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#2078

commit.message:
Migrate from per-map to per-texture .mat files

Each texture now has its own {texture_path}.mat file (e.g.
textures/common/quake/cop1_1.mat) rather than a shared per-map
maps/mapname.mat file. This mirrors how mesh models already handle
materials and enables far greater reuse across maps.

- Cm_LoadBspMaterials: loads textures/{name}.mat per texture
- R_LoadBspMaterials: drops map-level .mat preload
- R_LoadMaterial: tries textures/{name}.mat before suffix fallback
- R_SaveMaterials: writes per-texture .mat files for BSP models
- quemap AllocMaterial: seeds from per-texture .mat if present
- quemap LoadMaterials: iterates BSP materials, no per-map file
- quemap WriteMaterialsFile: non-destructive, per-texture output
- quemap MAT_Main: generates only missing per-texture .mat files
- quemap AddBspMaterials: bundles per-texture .mat and their assets
- qbsp/qlight: updated LoadMaterials() call sites

The transcoder Cloud Run Job was only getting a new image on deploy,
but CDN_BASE_URL env var was never updated. This caused all videos
transcoded after the variable change to get stale cdn.ai-teammate.me
URLs written to the DB instead of the correct GCS public URL.

on every deploy.

- 2 existing DB records (BB, El Dream) patched via SQL REPLACE

feat: popover component added (#28)

This PR adds a first-class `Popover` component to the UI library so
consumers can use a consistent, styled popover API instead of composing
Base UI primitives ad hoc.

- **New component: `Popover`**
- Added `/src/components/popover.tsx` as a wrapper over
`@base-ui/react/popover`.
  - Exposes a library-consistent API:
- root state/control props (`open`, `defaultOpen`, `onOpenChange`, etc.)
    - trigger passthrough + `trigger` render prop
    - composition slots: `portalProps`, `positionerProps`, `popupProps`
  - Includes shared subcomponents:
    - `PopoverTitle`
    - `PopoverDescription`
    - `PopoverClose`

- **Public API export**
- Exported from `/src/index.ts` so it is available to package consumers.

- **Storybook coverage**
  - Added `/src/stories/popover.stories.tsx` with:
    - `Basic` story
    - `Modal` story
  - Demonstrates common usage and generated docs for the new component.

- **Example usage**
  ```tsx
  <Popover trigger={<Button variant="outline">Open Popover</Button>}>
    <PopoverTitle>Popover content</PopoverTitle>
    <PopoverDescription>
      This popover can contain contextual information or actions.
    </PopoverDescription>
    <PopoverClose render={<Button size="sm">Close</Button>} />
  </Popover>
  ```

Generic session data API + roadmap as specialization

commit.message:
fix(server): use Cloud SQL socket factory instead of direct IP connection

- Switch JDBC URL to postgres-socket-factory (already in build.gradle)
  which connects via Cloud SQL Admin API (HTTPS/443) — no Authorized
  Networks or Auth Proxy sidecar needed
- Remove DB_HOST / DB_PORT from workflow env-vars (no longer used)
- Set CLOUD_SQL_INSTANCE=ai-native-478811:us-central1:learn-ai-db in workflow
- Add --add-cloudsql-instances flag to gcloud run deploy for IAM auth
- Reduce Hikari connection-timeout 20000ms → 5000ms (socket factory is fast;
  the 20s timeout was masking failures and causing 20-second response times)

Root cause: Cloud SQL had Authorized Networks=NONE, so direct TCP to
104.198.171.232:5432 was silently dropped → 20s HikariPool timeout on
every cold start and after connection resets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
